steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: $r$ identical objects have total mass $t$ kg, so each object has mass $\frac{t}{r}$ kg.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The total mass of $96r$ objects is $96r \times \frac{t}{r} = 96t$ kg.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate all four choices:
  • A: $96 - t$ — subtracts $t$ from 96, which has no physical meaning and does not scale with quantity. Eliminated.
  • B: $96 + t$ — adds $t$ to 96, also dimensionally inconsistent. Eliminated.
  • C: $\frac{t}{96}$ — divides by 96, giving a smaller mass for more objects. Eliminated.
  • D: $96t$ — multiplying the original total mass $t$ by 96 correctly scales to $96r$ objects. That is the correct answer.
